Anyway, I have the AC running in the background It's like 7,000 degrees in here. So hopefully I can clean that up But this is a bonus video and in this video, I want to share really what is a go-to Strategy for my business and for what we do with clients when we want to get the money Immediately if they have a database and that's something called a deadline Stack so I just showed you, you know the two main sequences, which is a quick sequence Hey, go look at this, you know, and then there's the countdown sequence Hey, you looked at that thing and mentioned an offer that offer expires, you know in two days or whatever Well, a deadline stack is where you take multiple countdown sequences and run them One after the other and I love these things. So typically what you'll do is Let's say for example, like right now You've got a webinar funnel or something right and you've got a database of people that's that have registered for your webinar Over time and it could be anything. I'm just using a webinar funnel as an example But let's say you've got a database of folks who registered for your webinar over time And some people attended some didn't some bought some didn't whatever right? Well, one of the strategies we like to deploy in that situation is first thing we'll do is we'll do a classic You know click sequence. Hey, go watch the webinar, right? And so literally we just stick the webinar video on a web page drive people to it We know most of them who go watch it They're not going to buy from that page immediately and then we do the standard playbook, right? Which is to put them into a countdown sequence But where this kind of thing can really pick up steam is when you do a deadline stack So let's say the thing you're selling on your webinar and incidentally, you know, it doesn't have to be a from a webinar It could be whatever it doesn't matter, but let's say the thing you're selling is like 300 bucks. All right, just kind of pulling that out of the air So deadline number one is hey, you know this thing's going to go away Registration is going to close in two days or whatever and you run a classic countdown sequence And it really goes away, right? So you honor that they can no longer go back to the original web page that they were going to to buy the thing now in a deadline stack What we'll do now is we'll wait two days three days however long you want to wait and then we'll reopen it and we'll create a separate web page It can be identical to the original one that was pitching the thing Reason we do that is so like the other one that expired really does expire, you know And then our email communication is essentially like hey We've decided to reopen this thing because we feel like it and so that's thing number one like never, you know There's never any need to make up some dumb story about why you're reopening the thing is because you want to so one of the reasons We'll give is we are reopening this thing because we want to and we've gotten a lot of good feedback from it And and this is where it starts to get different We've also decided to sweeten the pot by adding an additional bonus, right? And this whole offer expires in whatever two days, okay? So now like you have the original offer it's open back up But now you've added another bonus and then it really does expire, right? And maybe on your web page you like have at the very top of the web page You know you mentioned the new bonus or something sometimes we do if we're lazy We don't know we just mentioned it in the emails, okay? So then after that one we wait another two or three days We do the same process and now it's like hey, you know what man? We just done gone crazy over here And what we've decided to do is reopen this offer and remember that bonus we talked about earlier Well, we're still gonna give it to you But we've decided to sweeten the pot even more and add a second bonus But the catch is this is gonna expire in X amount of days, right? And it really does need to expire So that's why we always use for every deadline sequence We use a different web page because you really do want it to actually expire, right? And so you do that as many times as you want So however many bonuses you want to add, you know if you want to have like five of them You could literally stack five different deadlines together just so long as they actually do expire and they can't go back and buy from that original one The final one for us is always going to be you know what man? We decided to do one more thing because we're having so much fun with this and everybody's given us such great feedback is this You know, we had those three bonuses or whatever we were offering You know, well, we've decided to open that back up again So here's the deal for the next two days. You can still get the main thing AC just went off so I feel like I don't have to yell anymore for the next two days or however many days You can still get the main thing, right? The main course But and you can also still get the three bonuses But we've decided to make it even cooler and now instead of having to pay $300 You can get it for three payments of $100. So it's essentially like Deadline number one is things going away. Deadline number two is new bonus added Deadline number three is second bonus added. So like if you have a database and you have a thing anything You can literally just run a deadline stack to it right now And get immediate money and then of course you would integrate that into your sales process from this day forward So kind of a long intro to this concept, but I wanted you to get the big picture So now I'm gonna do is I'm going to through the magic of editing Walk you through the actual deployment of these and some copy strategies, etc Here we go. Frank Kern video editing mastery in three two one and By far I think the most effective type of email sequence and promotion that uses email is going to be the deadline But your client might get weirded out about too much email So whenever I prescribe this if there is any sort of resistance, which there isn't often But in the event there is it's because the client gets weirded out You know the client don't have a belief that the person doesn't buy immediately because they don't want what the offer is or whatever And the fact of the matter is it's just usually not right A lot of times people don't buy something because they're just procrastinating and I found a classic example of this So what you see right here? It's obviously a little bit older But I have to take like, you know all these medications and still not a time or anything But you know I have to take medications regularly and I live probably three miles from the pharmacy and so they'll Text you you know when you're when your medicine is is ready then I couldn't get all of the texts on the one screen, but essentially what you see here is CVS having the text me multiple times to come get the medicine that I've already like had called in you know and gone to the doctor to get prescribed to me and everything and I'm just procrastinated so much if you look at this bottom one That's like hey, we we can hold it for four more days you know, but then we're gonna get rid of this dang thing and I do that all the time like I always end up picking it up on like the last 24 hour period that I possibly can and it's because of Procrastination right so one of the things you really got to make clear to a client and and also internally just understand is that One reason people don't buy stuff is They're just putting it off. You know they get distracted. It's not necessarily because they want it So what is it that makes me get off my butt and go the whopping three miles to the pharmacy? This is the fact that if I don't go that deadline is gonna expire and now I gotta call the doctor again And get them to call the prescription back in there's a pain in the ass, right? So it's a classic example of deadlines working outside of the marketing context So I had this idea of like what if like we could really make deadlines a lot stronger by stacking them on top of each other and I did it and it turns out that it's like I don't want to say it's like a miracle cure or something that would be a gross overstatement, but it's in Ridiculously powerful way to increase revenue for a client almost immediately assuming it's the appropriate thing to prescribe So the deadline stack is this Generally, you've got three deadline sequences that happen for the same product one after the other All right, so deadline sequence number one would be a price or bonus based deadline You know like a classic like think of a 4-day cash machine structure You know like hey, you can get a discount on this thing and to get a bonus But it expires in the next days and that's always worked really really well and Then I was like well, you know what what happened if after that deadline expired You wait a couple days and then you go back to the prospect base and you say you know what I Decided to do this again like why not? You know and this time What you do is you know you have your client can keep the same price or have it raise a little bit or whatever But essentially you want to say In a perfect scenario. Hey, you know this has been so cool and everyone's just enjoying this promotion so much I've decided to open it back up for another X amount of days But the same discount or at the same price or whatever and I'm gonna include that extra That original bonus that I promised you but you know, I've decided to add a second bonus too Right and so now we're basically stacking bonuses So if you think about it like a lot of times you'll just stack bonuses in one deadline sequence Which is the classic 4-day cash machine structure So now what we're doing is we're doing a bonus stack in conjunction with multiple deadline stacks So that deadline expires right and all of these they really do need to expire I don't think one should use the false scarcity angle. It's not really, you know that cool So now your third deadline sequence would be if the person still doesn't buy is Both bonuses are still available and the payment can be the same or whatever you want to do But usually I like to make it more appealing by adding a payment plan So if bonus number one here was okay, you can have the thing for you know, it's normally $3,000 You can have it for $1,000 plus you get this free bonus, but it expires in two days. That's deadline number one Deadline number two after that expired you wait a couple days and say, you know what the feedback has been so great This has really worked out wonderfully. I'm enjoying it. I've decided to reopen this promotion for another couple days You can still get it for $1,000. You can still have that bonus But you know what? I'm also going to give you a second bonus just to be cool Right and so that's the third or the second and then that expires You wait a couple of days if the prospect still hasn't purchased the third deadline sequence to be like, you know what? The feedback has been so great from this. I'm having so much fun doing all this I've decided to open this promotion up again You can still have these two bonuses that are awesome and you know what on top of that I've gotten a lot of requests So you see where it says deadline one final day and that day I like to send six emails and this is where clients can get like oh my gosh are you crazy? So what I recommend to you is to say yeah, I am crazy and let's try it and you know see what happens and if People get mad and we won't do it anymore But I've never had anyone not want to do it anymore and the reason there of course is because you're only sending it to people that Have behaviorally indicated they're interested right like You're not gonna go register for a webinar stay till the end see the pitch if you're not intensely interested in the thing being sold Same thing if you go watch the SL same thing if you go read along form sales letter So you don't do this to an entire list out of nowhere, you know what I mean So anyway now you look and did they buy it of course? So here's some more questions right so we already addressed where you should send them Then you might have a question of when your client doesn't have any bonuses Okay, so you got a couple of things you could do number one is you could refer to the AOV increase frameworks and Questionnaires and stuff that we went over last week and they could just make them You know, it gets worth the extra effort just to make a bonus To give away with something even if his e-com, you know, and they don't want to Give away like inventory with a hard cost or whatever the bonus could be free shipping You know and you can even be creative like I've got a client who would bonus a dog training download For clients who were purchasing e-com products about a removing pet stains Yeah, also, there's no there's no additional hard cost there for them So it's like there can always be some sort of bonus created Some clients are unable to offer payment terms like for whatever reason they can't break the payments You know, like if it was a thousand dollars hypothetically It's for whatever reason they can't do 10 payments of a hundred, you know And who knows why they couldn't but let's just say it's a legitimate reason In that case what you would want to do is just add a third bonus, you know Pretty simple, right? Another one would be like well, what if you don't want to do a discount? So if you don't want to discount the product then you can Overcome that with two things number one removing the ability to purchase the product altogether when the deadlines are over and Adding more bonuses, you know, so there's a really never underestimate the value of premiums And really what you're doing by the way is you're just getting the offer in front of the audience more frequently with another reason to present the offer Because your your client and to a degree us or you know ourselves we do this too We have this belief that our prospects are reading every single email. They're actually paying attention and they're not So they could totally zone out on deadline sequence one deadline sequence number two It just happened to start reading along in deadline sequence number three, you know So it's just another way to keep their attention And then the fifth one which it comes up sometimes or not really and that's okay What if people become conditioned to this and they just wait until the end of the bonuses and what I can tell you is I've just never seen it happen Especially in my own business. I'm a year 20 and I do deadlines and stuff like this all the time and it's never been an issue and the reason that has never been an issue is because Our prospects and your clients prospects they do not pay nearly as much attention to us as we wish they did You know, they're not just looking at our every move going. Oh, when's this gonna happen? You know, I mean you might have one or two or something But if you do you're really lucky that you've got that kind of attention from your marketplace With in most cases we just don't so it's if your client asks you that I would say, you know chances are they're not going to But why don't we measure and see and if you see any negative impact from this we could do something else But generally speaking in fact not generally speaking for me literally every single time I've had someone deploy this all of these questions are no longer relevant to them Once they've deployed just even the first one because this works so extremely well. So this one's really really powerful It when you go back through like the materials we've got You'll see as you're going through my swipe files that are included with this lesson You'll see that it's just a matter of using a countdown sequence framework I call countdown sequences and deadline sequences the same thing But you get what I mean this using a countdown sequence email framework and the only changes in the copy are the references to the new bonuses And so that's it.
